blocks.heading: The three pure-authoring products — render a hand-written spec, doc, or quiz straight into self-contained HTML, with no codebase scan.

rows.value: 10 — three thin CLIs over `src/` (two render paths, two block models, two lint floors, one question renderer)

Unlike `recap` / `atlas`, the input is **hand-authored blocks JSON**, not a scan. `spec` renders an approval-focused design spec; `doc` is the general illustrated-doc renderer; `quiz` renders a comprehension check that verifies a reader understood a change.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.line: "Three thin CLIs over the shared engine."
bigIdea.sub: `doc` reuses **core's generic `Block` model** directly — `bin/doc.ts` just promotes mermaid fences and calls `assemble`, with almost no domain code of its own. `spec` adds its **own approval-focused block vocabulary** (`SpecBlock` — tldr, decisions, scope, rollout, approve) plus a dedicated `assembleSpec` renderer and a completeness `lintSpec` floor. `quiz` follows `spec`'s pattern exactly: its own vocabulary (`QuizBlock` — family-tagged questions, theme groups, structured citations), a dedicated `assembleQuiz` renderer (plus the `quiz-question` block renderer), and a structural `lintQuiz` floor. All three ride the same diagram pipeline.

blocks.title: The pieces
blocks.intro: `doc` is the thin one — it leans on core + diagrams with no domain-specific model. `spec` and `quiz` are the substantial ones: each has its own block model, its own renderer, and its own lint. Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: spec CLI
cards.purpose: `bin/spec.ts` — parses `--blocks <spec.json> --out <dir>`, maps page-level fields to `SpecOpts`, calls `assembleSpec`, and writes `spec.html` + re-writes `spec.json` so the folder re-renders in place.

cards.name: doc CLI
cards.purpose: `bin/doc.ts` — the thinner CLI: reads `--blocks` as core's generic `Block[]`, promotes mermaid fences, calls core's `assemble`, writes `doc.html` + `blocks.json`. No spec model, no lint.

cards.name: spec block model
cards.purpose: `src/spec-blocks.ts` — the `SpecBlock` union: the approval vocabulary (`tldr`, `decisions`, `scope`, `rollout`, `approve`, `risks`, `done`, `reference`, …) plus id-uniqueness, chapter labelling, and diagram collection.

cards.name: spec render
cards.purpose: `src/assemble-spec.ts` — `assembleSpec` renders each block to a `<section>`, derives the sidebar outline + progress rail from the chapters, and reuses the recap's `renderDiagramCard` for diagrams.

cards.name: spec lint
cards.purpose: `src/lint-spec.ts` — the demo-standard completeness floor: warns (never crashes) when the lead, decisions, or scope are missing, scaling stricter for large specs.

cards.name: quiz CLI
cards.purpose: `bin/quiz.ts` — parses `--blocks <quiz.json> --out <dir>`, maps the `QuizDoc` envelope to `QuizOpts`, calls `assembleQuiz`, and writes `quiz.html` + re-writes `quiz.json`. Assembly runs in a try/catch that flushes buffered warnings before rethrowing.

cards.name: quiz block model
cards.purpose: `src/quiz-blocks.ts` — the `QuizBlock` union (`quiz-question`, `quiz-group`, prose) plus the `QuizDoc` envelope, structured `Citation`s, nested-id uniqueness (incl. attached diagram/code ids), and diagram collection.

cards.name: quiz render
cards.purpose: `src/assemble-quiz.ts` + `src/renderers/quiz-question.ts` — `assembleQuiz` builds the quiz shell (questions sidebar, TL;DR fold, theme toggle); `renderQuizQuestion` draws each question with a family badge, optional code/diagram, a native `<details>` reveal, and text citations.

cards.name: quiz lint
cards.purpose: `src/lint-quiz.ts` — the structural floor: question count, model answers, citations, trivia patterns, family mix and unknown families, dangling citation fragments. Altitude and proportionality stay authoring rules in the skill.

blocks.note: `doc` has no card of its own in `src/` — it borrows core's `assemble` and the diagrams domain's `promoteMermaidFences` wholesale. The four spec pieces and the four quiz pieces (`bin/` CLI + model + renderer + lint each) are what this domain actually owns; `renderers/quiz-question.ts` sits in `src/renderers/` but is quiz-specific, so it lives here rather than in core.

blocks.title: Three entrypoints, one core
blocks.intro: `spec.json` flows through `bin/spec.ts` → `assembleSpec` (using `spec-blocks` + `lintSpec`); `quiz.json` flows through `bin/quiz.ts` → `assembleQuiz` (using `quiz-blocks` + `lintQuiz`); `blocks.json` flows through `bin/doc.ts` → core's `assemble`. All three share the diagrams pipeline and the core renderers.

diagram.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  specjson["spec.json (authored blocks)"] --> specbin["bin/spec.ts"]
  specbin --> asmspec["assembleSpec"]
  asmspec -->|blocks + lint| specmodel["spec-blocks + lint-spec"]
  asmspec --> spechtml["spec.html"]
  quizjson["quiz.json (authored blocks)"] --> quizbin["bin/quiz.ts"]
  quizbin --> asmquiz["assembleQuiz"]
  asmquiz -->|blocks + lint| quizmodel["quiz-blocks + lint-quiz"]
  asmquiz --> quizhtml["quiz.html"]
  blocksjson["blocks.json (authored blocks)"] --> docbin["bin/doc.ts"]
  docbin -->|promote fences| promote["promote-mermaid (diagrams)"]
  promote --> asmcore["core / assemble"]
  asmcore --> dochtml["doc.html"]
  asmspec -->|shares renderers| asmcore
  asmquiz -->|shares renderers| asmcore
  classDef changed fill:#ffd43b,stroke:#f08c00,color:#1b1b1b,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef actor fill:#d0ebff,stroke:#4dabf7,color:#1b1b1b;
  classDef store fill:#e5dbff,stroke:#9775fa,color:#1b1b1b;
  class specjson,quizjson,blocksjson store;
  class specbin,quizbin,docbin actor;
  class asmspec,asmquiz changed;

blocks.callout: The amber boxes — `assembleSpec` and `assembleQuiz` — are the two substantial renderers authoring owns. Everything below them (`core / assemble`, the diagram pipeline) is borrowed; `bin/doc.ts` is just wiring.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per piece — what it does, its files and exports, and how it connects to core, diagrams, and recap.

components.name: spec CLI
detail: `bin/spec.ts` is the visual-spec entrypoint. It `parseArgs` for `--blocks <spec.json>` and `--out <dir>` (both required; relative paths resolve against the cwd), reads the JSON as a `SpecDoc` (page options + a `blocks` array), maps the page-level fields onto `SpecOpts`, and calls [`assembleSpec`](#c-spec-render). It writes `<out>/spec.html` and re-writes `<out>/spec.json`, so the doc folder stays self-contained and re-renders in place — mirroring `recap --blocks`. Author warnings collected via `onWarn` are printed after the write.

files.name: bin/spec.ts
files.desc: Arg parsing, `SpecDoc` → `SpecOpts` mapping, and the write-back of `spec.html` + `spec.json`.

exports.name: main()
exports.desc: The CLI body — reads, assembles, writes; exits 2 on usage errors.

exports.name: SpecDoc
exports.desc: `Partial<SpecOpts> & { blocks: SpecBlock[] }` — the on-disk `spec.json` shape.

components.name: doc CLI
detail: `bin/doc.ts` is the thinnest product in the toolkit. It reads `--blocks` as core's **generic `Block[]`** (no spec model involved), runs [`promoteMermaidFences`](../domain-diagrams/domain-diagrams.html) to lift ```mermaid``` code fences into real diagram blocks, then hands the result to core's [`assemble`](../domain-core/domain-core.html#c-assemble). It writes `doc.html` plus a copy of `blocks.json` into the `--out` directory so the doc is self-contained and re-renders in place. There is no `assemble-doc`, no `doc-blocks`, and no lint — doc is wiring over core + diagrams.

files.name: bin/doc.ts
files.desc: Reads `Block[]`, promotes mermaid fences, calls core `assemble`, writes `doc.html` + `blocks.json`.

exports.name: main()
exports.desc: The CLI body — promote + assemble + write; throws on missing `--blocks`.

connections.body: core's generic `Block` model directly — no domain-specific block types

components.name: spec block model
detail: The `SpecBlock` discriminated union — the approval-focused vocabulary that distinguishes a spec from a generic doc: `tldr` (the lead), `diagram` (info-flow hero / where-it-fits), `components` (with the optional two-layer anatomy explainer), `fits`, `decisions` (the highest-value content for an approver), `scope` (in/out boundaries), `rollout` (gated phases), `done`, `risks`, `approve` (the commit / scrutinize / open-questions band), `reference` (drill-down drawers), and the `spec-prose` escape hatch. Helpers derive the page structure: `isChapter` (tldr + reference are not numbered chapters), `assertUniqueSpecIds`, `chapterLabel`, and `collectSpecDiagrams` / `toDiagramBlock` to feed the shared diagram pipeline.

files.name: spec-blocks.ts
files.desc: The `SpecBlock` union, every block interface, and the chapter / id / diagram helpers.

exports.name: SpecBlock
exports.desc: The block union — `TldrBlock | DecisionsBlock | ScopeBlock | RolloutBlock | ApproveBlock | …`.

exports.name: collectSpecDiagrams()
exports.desc: Gathers every `diagram` block as core `DiagramBlock`s for the shared pipeline.

exports.name: toDiagramBlock()
exports.desc: The `DiagramBlock` view of one spec diagram section (same id).

exports.name: isChapter()
exports.desc: With `chapterLabel()`, drives the numbered outline and the progress rail.

exports.name: assertUniqueSpecIds()
exports.desc: Id-collision detection across blocks and reference items (throws).

connections.body: core's generic `Block` model that [doc CLI](#c-doc-cli) uses

components.name: spec render
detail: `assembleSpec(blocks, opts)` is the spec page builder. It asserts unique ids, loads `review.css` + `spec.css` + the shared viewer JS + the dark-mode toggle assets (`theme-head.js`, `theme-toggle.js`, `theme.css`), collects the spec diagrams and renders them via the diagrams domain's `renderAll`, then runs the page through `validateSpecOpts` and [`lintSpec`](#c-spec-lint) (warnings only). Each block becomes a `<section>` via a per-type renderer; the sidebar outline and the progress rail are derived from the chapters (the rail is injected right after the TL;DR). For diagram blocks it reuses the **recap's** `renderDiagramCard` — the one piece of review-shell rendering authoring borrows.

files.name: assemble-spec.ts
files.desc: Topbar / sidebar / rail, the per-block `<section>` renderers, and the page shell.

exports.name: assembleSpec()
exports.desc: → a full self-contained HTML string for the spec page.

exports.name: SpecOpts
exports.desc: Page options: title, phase, status, date, complexity, related, meta, outDir, excalidraw.

components.name: spec lint
detail: `lintSpec(blocks)` is the demo-standard completeness floor — a mechanical backstop that keeps every spec at the bar. It returns warning strings (never throws): no `tldr` lead, an empty TL;DR, missing `decisions` (the load-bearing content for an approver) or a decision lacking a `why`, and a missing `scope` block. For a *large* spec (≥ 5 chapters) it also expects a TL;DR big-idea line, a hero `diagram`, a `rollout`, and an `approve` band; independently of size, four-plus decisions with no named rejected-alternative earn a nudge to name the path not taken on the contested ones. `assembleSpec` surfaces these through `onWarn`.

files.name: lint-spec.ts
files.desc: The heuristic checks (lead / decisions / scope, scaled stricter at `LARGE_CHAPTERS = 5`).

exports.name: lintSpec()
exports.desc: → `string[]` of completeness warnings, in document priority order.

connections.body: heuristics, not hard errors — the page still renders, the author is nudged

components.name: quiz CLI
detail: `bin/quiz.ts` is the quiz entrypoint, mirroring `bin/spec.ts`. It `parseArgs` for `--blocks <quiz.json>` and `--out <dir>` (relative paths resolve against the cwd), reads the `QuizDoc` envelope, maps its page fields onto `QuizOpts`, and calls [`assembleQuiz`](#c-quiz-render). It writes `<out>/quiz.html` and re-writes `<out>/quiz.json` so the folder re-renders in place. One deliberate difference from its siblings: assembly runs inside a try/catch that flushes the buffered warnings *before* rethrowing, so a malformed `quiz.json` still surfaces its structural lint warnings instead of dying silently.

files.name: bin/quiz.ts
files.desc: Arg parsing, `QuizDoc` → `QuizOpts` mapping, warning flush on crash, and the write-back of `quiz.html` + `quiz.json`.

exports.name: main()
exports.desc: The CLI body — reads, assembles, writes; exits 2 on usage errors.

components.name: quiz block model
detail: The `QuizBlock` discriminated union — a comprehension-check vocabulary kept deliberately separate from core's generic `Block` and spec's `SpecBlock`: `quiz-question` (a family-tagged question — system-fit / rationale / mechanism — with a markdown prompt, an optional attached `annotated-code` / `diagram` primitive, a model answer, and structured `Citation`s) and `quiz-group` (a theme wrapper for large quizzes), composing with core's `prose`. The `QuizDoc` envelope carries title / source / intro. Helpers: `allQuestions` and `allBlockIds` (the valid citation-fragment targets), `assertUniqueQuizIds` (including attached diagram/code ids — duplicates would silently collapse in the render map), and `collectQuizDiagrams` for the shared pipeline.

files.name: quiz-blocks.ts
files.desc: The `QuizBlock` union, the `QuizDoc` envelope, `Citation`, and the question / id / diagram helpers.

exports.name: QuizBlock
exports.desc: The block union — `QuizQuestionBlock | QuizGroupBlock | ProseBlock`.

exports.name: QuizDoc
exports.desc: The on-disk `quiz.json` envelope — title, source, intro, blocks.

exports.name: allQuestions()
exports.desc: Questions in document order, descending into groups.

exports.name: assertUniqueQuizIds()
exports.desc: Id-collision detection across blocks, group children, and attached diagram/code ids (throws).

exports.name: collectQuizDiagrams()
exports.desc: Gathers attached question diagrams for the shared pipeline.

components.name: quiz render
detail: `assembleQuiz(blocks, opts)` builds the quiz page: it asserts unique ids, runs [`lintQuiz`](#c-quiz-lint) *before* diagram rendering (so structural warnings survive a diagram crash), renders attached diagrams via the diagrams domain's `renderAll`, and assembles the shell — topbar, a questions sidebar whose numbering is derived from the same traversal as the body, a TL;DR fold with per-family counts, group sections that render question *and* prose children, and the shared dark-mode theme assets. `renderQuizQuestion` — the one quiz renderer that lives in `src/renderers/` — draws each question: family badge, prompt, optional code/diagram, a native `<details>` "Reveal answer", and citations rendered as styled text. Only a `fragment` citation validated against the doc's own block ids becomes a link (the safe-link policy: no relative or file hrefs).

files.name: assemble-quiz.ts
files.desc: The quiz shell — topbar / sidebar / TL;DR fold, group rendering, lint-before-render ordering, theme assets.

files.name: renderers/quiz-question.ts
files.desc: Per-question rendering: family badge, `<details>` reveal, citation text/links.

exports.name: assembleQuiz()
exports.desc: → a full self-contained HTML string for the quiz page.

exports.name: QuizOpts
exports.desc: Page options: title, source, intro, outDir, excalidraw, generator, onWarn.

exports.name: renderQuizQuestion()
exports.desc: One question → HTML, with validated citation fragments.

connections.body: [quiz lint](#c-quiz-lint) before diagram rendering (warnings, never fatal)

components.name: quiz lint
detail: `lintQuiz(blocks)` is the structural floor, mirroring `lintSpec`'s philosophy (warning strings, never throws): fewer than 2 questions, a question missing its model-answer takeaway or citations, trivia-pattern prompts (`which file` / `how many` / `what line` / `did we test`), an unknown `family` value, a citation `fragment` that resolves to no block id, and a medium-plus quiz (`MEDIUM_QUIZ = 4`) drawn entirely from a single family. Altitude and proportionality are deliberately *not* linted — they are authoring rules in the quiz skill, because the render-only CLI has no source inventory to judge coverage against.

files.name: lint-quiz.ts
files.desc: The heuristic checks (count / answers / citations / trivia / family mix / fragments).

exports.name: lintQuiz()
exports.desc: → `string[]` of structural warnings.

connections.body: [quiz render](#c-quiz-render) — emitted via `opts.onWarn`, before diagram rendering
[quiz block model](#c-quiz-model) — questions, families, citations, fragment targets
heuristics, not hard errors — the page still renders, the author is nudged

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: Authoring owns **two block models** — the approval vocabulary in `spec-blocks.ts` and the comprehension vocabulary in `quiz-blocks.ts`. `doc` owns no model of its own; it borrows core's generic `Block`.

rows.name: TldrBlock
rows.desc: `type: "tldr"` — the lead: a one-line heading, What / Why / Closes / Size rows, and an optional big-idea line.

rows.name: SpecDiagramBlock
rows.desc: `type: "diagram"` — a standalone d2/mermaid section (info-flow hero, where-it-fits); its id is both section and diagram id.

rows.name: ComponentsBlock · ComponentCard
rows.desc: `type: "components"` — the component grid, with the optional two-layer anatomy (factual / perceived) explainer.

rows.name: FitsBlock
rows.desc: `type: "fits"` — the where-it-fits chain plus an optional new-vs-reused layer stack.

rows.name: DecisionsBlock · DecisionItem
rows.desc: `type: "decisions"` — Q / A / why / rejected cards; the highest-value content for an approver.

rows.name: ScopeBlock
rows.desc: `type: "scope"` — in-scope goals vs out-of-scope anti-goals, with optional defer targets.

rows.name: RolloutBlock · PhaseItem
rows.desc: `type: "rollout"` — gated sub-phases, each with a scope and an acceptance gate.

rows.name: ApproveBlock
rows.desc: `type: "approve"` — the sign-off band: what you're approving, what to scrutinize, and open non-blocking questions.

rows.name: ReferenceBlock · SpecProseBlock
rows.desc: `type: "reference"` (collapsed drill-down drawers) and `type: "spec-prose"` (the unmodeled-content escape hatch).

rows.name: QuizDoc
rows.desc: the `quiz.json` envelope — title, source (what was quizzed), intro, and the `QuizBlock[]`.

rows.name: QuizQuestionBlock
rows.desc: `type: "quiz-question"` — a family-tagged question (system-fit / rationale / mechanism): markdown prompt, optional attached code/diagram, model answer (takeaway + points), citations.

rows.name: QuizGroupBlock
rows.desc: `type: "quiz-group"` — a theme wrapper for large quizzes; children are questions and prose.

rows.name: Citation
rows.desc: structured grounding for a model answer — label, optional file/lines, optional in-page `fragment` (the only linkable form).

blocks.note: The spec rows compose into `SpecBlock`; the quiz rows into `QuizBlock`. Borrows (never owns): `DiagramBlock` / `DiagramKind` / `TabsBlock` plus `ProseBlock` / `AnnotatedCodeBlock` from `core`; the generic `Block` model that `doc` renders.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: Three CLIs out; the shared engine, the diagram pipeline, and one recap shell piece in.

exposes.note: the spec page builder, used by the CLI
the quiz page builder, used by the CLI

blocks.note: `doc` leans entirely on `core` (`assemble`, `Block`) and `diagrams` (`promoteMermaidFences`). `spec` and `quiz` each add their own model + renderer + lint, and both reuse exactly one review-shell piece: the recap's `renderDiagramCard` for their diagram cards.
